text.title: OpenAPI Specification Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the OpenAPI Specification methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Repo MUST contain exactly one canonical openapi.yaml at root; distribution uses `redocly bundle`; split/dereferenced copies in source tree are forbidden.

rationale.source: Redocly bundle workflow; OpenAPI Initiative guidance

rule.rationale: Multiple copies diverge immediately; one source + one bundle command keeps consumers in sync.

rule.statement: Every operation MUST carry a unique `operationId` in kebab-case or camelCase; missing or duplicate IDs are rejected.
rule.rationale: Generators key client method names off operationId; absence produces verbose path-based names that break on rename.

rule.statement: All reusable schemas, parameters, and responses MUST live under `components/*` and be referenced via `$ref`; inline duplication is rejected.
rule.rationale: Duplicated schemas drift; only the components version is updated and inlined copies stay stale, producing incorrect generated clients.

rule.statement: Every `type: object` schema MUST declare an explicit `required` array listing non-optional properties; missing `required` arrays are rejected by Spectral.
rule.rationale: Generators treat all properties as optional when required is absent, producing client code that compiles but fails at runtime on missing fields.

rule.statement: Every PR MUST run `oasdiff breaking` against the main branch; any BREAK classification requires a documented human override.
rule.rationale: Breaking changes (path removal, required-field addition, enum shrinkage) silently break existing clients; automated detection prevents accidental merges.

rule.statement: Every response code MUST include at least one named `examples` entry; raw `example:` keys without a name are rejected.
rule.rationale: Named examples let clients and tests reference specific scenarios (e.g. `UserNotFound`) rather than parsing generic example objects.

rule.statement: When the spec is server-generated (FastAPI, drf-spectacular, NestJS), the committed `openapi.yaml` MUST be regenerated in CI and the build fails on diff.
rule.rationale: Generated specs drift from the committed snapshot when developers change models without regenerating; CI drift detection prevents the docs from lying.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec - decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
